Defines the main contract for authorization implementations."},

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/authorizer/AclCreateResult.java", "symbol": "AclCreateResult", "type": "class", "description": "Result of an ACL create operation, containing optional exception."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/authorizer/AclDeleteResult.java", "symbol": "AclDeleteResult", "type": "class", "description": "Result of an ACL delete operation with deletion results for each matching binding."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/ClusterMetadataAuthorizer.java", "symbol": "ClusterMetadataAuthorizer", "type": "interface", "description": "Interface for Authorizers which store state in the __cluster_metadata log. Extends Authorizer."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/StandardAuthorizer.java", "symbol": "StandardAuthorizer", "type": "class", "description": "Built-in authorizer implementation that stores ACLs in the metadata log. Implements ClusterMetadataAuthorizer and Monitorable."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/AclMutator.java", "symbol": "AclMutator", "type": "interface", "description": "Interface for ACL create and delete operations, implemented by QuorumController."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/StandardAcl.java", "symbol": "StandardAcl", "type": "class", "description": "Represents a Kafka ACL identified by UUID and stored in the metadata log."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/StandardAclWithId.java", "symbol": "StandardAclWithId", "type": "class", "description": "Tuple of (id, acl) representing an ACL with its UUID identifier."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/AclCache.java", "symbol": "AclCache", "type": "class", "description": "Immutable class that stores ACLs in KRaft-based clusters, indexed by resource and UUID."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/StandardAuthorizerData.java", "symbol": "StandardAuthorizerData", "type": "class", "description": "Encapsulates configuration and ACL data owned by StandardAuthorizer."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/StandardAclRecordIterator.java", "symbol": "StandardAclRecordIterator", "type": "class", "description": "Iterator for ACL records that batches them for efficient serialization."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/security/authorizer/AuthorizerUtils.java", "symbol": "AuthorizerUtils", "type": "class", "description": "Utility class for creating and initializing authorizer instances."},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489", "path": "metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/publisher/AclPublisher.java", "symbol": "AclPublisher", "type": "class", "description": "MetadataPublisher implementation that publishes ACL updates to ClusterMetadataAuthorizer instances."}
  ],
  "text": "This audit found 17 Java source files implementing or defining Access Control List (ACL) authorization logic in Apache Kafka. The files are organized across three main packages:\n\n**Core Authorizer Interface (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/authorizer/)**:\nThe Authorizer interface is the main pluggable contract for Kafka broker authorization. It defines methods for authorization (authorize), ACL management (createAcls, deleteAcls), and metadata querying (acls). Supporting interfaces include AuthorizerServerInfo (broker metadata), AuthorizableRequestContext (request context), and supporting classes like Action (what to authorize), AuthorizationResult (enum), AclCreateResult, and AclDeleteResult.\n\n**Cluster Metadata Authorizer (metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/)**:\nThe ClusterMetadataAuthorizer interface extends Authorizer for KRaft-based clusters where ACLs are stored in the __cluster_metadata log. StandardAuthorizer is the built-in implementation of ClusterMetadataAuthorizer that stores ACLs in metadata. It uses:\n- AclMutator interface: For controller-based ACL creation/deletion\n- StandardAcl: Represents individual ACLs with resource, principal, operation, and permission\n- StandardAclWithId: Pairs ACLs with their UUID identifiers\n- AclCache: Immutable cache structure maintaining ACLs indexed by resource and UUID\n- StandardAuthorizerData: Encapsulates authorizer configuration and cached ACL data\n- StandardAclRecordIterator: Batches ACL records for efficient serialization\n\n**Supporting Components**:\n- AuthorizerUtils (server/src/main/java): Factory utility for creating authorizer instances\n- AclPublisher (metadata/src/main/java): MetadataPublisher that propagates ACL changes from metadata log to authorizer instances\n\nThe architecture supports both pluggable authorizer implementations via the Authorizer interface and the built-in StandardAuthorizer for KRaft clusters with cluster-metadata-based ACL storage."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/camel--1006f047` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/camel--1006f047$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/sg-evals/camel--1006f047`, `github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc`, `github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489`

# Kafka ACL Authorization Code Audit

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in `apache/kafka` that implement or define Access Control List (ACL) authorization logic. Search under `core/src/main/` and `clients/src/main/` for files containing classes or interfaces with `Authorizer` or `AclAuthorizer` in the name. For each file, report the class name and whether it defines the interface, implements it, or provides test utilities.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` (apache/kafka)
- `sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` (apache/flink)
- `sg-evals/camel--1006f047` (apache/camel)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Java source files in the Kafka repository that implement or define ACL authorization logic. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for files with `Authorizer` or `AclAuthorizer` in their names.
Let me search more systematically for all ACL and Authorizer files in the core and clients directories.
Now let me read the other files in the authorizer packages to get a complete picture.
Now I'll compile my findings into the requested JSON format.
Let me verify the answer was created successfully:

Perfect! I've completed the Kafka ACL Authorization Code Audit and created the comprehensive answer file at `/workspace/answer.json`.

## Summary

I found **17 Java source files** implementing or defining Access Control List (ACL) authorization logic in Apache Kafka:

**Core Interfaces & Supporting Classes (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/authorizer/)**:
- **Authorizer** - Main pluggable authorizer interface defining the contract for broker authorization
- **AuthorizerServerInfo** - Runtime broker configuration metadata interface
- **AuthorizableRequestContext** - Request context interface providing authentication/connection info
- **Action**, **AuthorizationResult**, **AclCreateResult**, **AclDeleteResult** - Supporting classes for authorization operations

**Cluster Metadata Authorizer Implementation (metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/)**:
- **ClusterMetadataAuthorizer** - Interface extending Authorizer for KRaft-based clusters
- **StandardAuthorizer** - Built-in implementation storing ACLs in the metadata log
- **AclMutator** - Interface for ACL creation/deletion operations
- **StandardAcl**, **StandardAclWithId**, **AclCache**, **StandardAuthorizerData**, **StandardAclRecordIterator** - Supporting data structures and utilities

**Supporting Components**:
- **AuthorizerUtils** - Factory utility for creating authorizer instances
- **AclPublisher** - MetadataPublisher propagating ACL changes to authorizers

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed symbol information and a comprehensive narrative explanation of the architecture and relationships between these components.

  "text": "This audit found 17 Java source files implementing or defining Access Control List (ACL) authorization logic in Apache Kafka. The files are organized across three main packages:\n\n**Core Authorizer Interface (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/authorizer/)**:\nThe Authorizer interface is the main pluggable contract for Kafka broker authorization. It defines methods for authorization (authorize), ACL management (createAcls, deleteAcls), and metadata querying (acls). Supporting interfaces include AuthorizerServerInfo (broker metadata), AuthorizableRequestContext (request context), and supporting classes like Action (what to authorize), AuthorizationResult (enum), AclCreateResult, and AclDeleteResult.\n\n**Cluster Metadata Authorizer (metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/authorizer/)**:\nThe ClusterMetadataAuthorizer interface extends Authorizer for KRaft-based clusters where ACLs are stored in the __cluster_metadata log. StandardAuthorizer is the built-in implementation of ClusterMetadataAuthorizer that stores ACLs in metadata. It uses:\n- AclMutator interface: For controller-based ACL creation/deletion\n- StandardAcl: Represents individual ACLs with resource, principal, operation, and permission\n- StandardAclWithId: Pairs ACLs with their UUID identifiers\n- AclCache: Immutable cache structure maintaining ACLs indexed by resource and UUID\n- StandardAuthorizerData: Encapsulates authorizer configuration and cached ACL data\n- StandardAclRecordIterator: Batches ACL records for efficient serialization\n\n**Supporting Components**:\n- AuthorizerUtils (server/src/main/java): Factory utility for creating authorizer instances\n- AclPublisher (metadata/src/main/java): MetadataPublisher that propagates ACL changes from metadata log to authorizer instances\n\nThe architecture supports both pluggable authorizer implementations via the Authorizer interface and the built-in StandardAuthorizer for KRaft clusters with cluster-metadata-based ACL storage."
